rules_applied.RULE7: Does NOT fire based on model (QAT 22.63% > 15%). BUT market prices QAT at 12.6% (below 15%). Apply near-RULE7 caution for Qatar player props.
rules_applied.RULE8: FIRES — Draw 29.76% > 25%. HOWEVER: both teams need to WIN for 3rd-place advancement. This reduces the effective draw probability (market says 18.2%). RULE8's 'cagey match' assumption is LESS applicable here — both teams attack.
rules_applied.RULE15: Near-applicable for QAT forwards. Market QAT win 12.6% is in RULE15 territory. Apply moderate SOT suppression to Qatar attacker props.
rules_applied.FLOOR_0.25: Applied to Q8 (Qatar 2+ offsides) — Qatar had 0 offsides in GD1 (90 min), very limited attacking runs. Floor binds.
rules_applied.RULE_FOULS: BIH expected to dominate possession and attack. QAT defends → QAT fouls more. Direction: Qatar more fouls.
rules_applied.SURVIVAL_MATCH_CORNERS: Partially applicable — both teams need to WIN (not draw) for advancement. This is motivation-wise similar to a survival match. However the stakes are slightly lower (3rd place chance, not elimination). Treat Q1 (corners) with moderate caution but less extreme than full survival match.
rules_applied.BOTH_TEAMS_MUST_WIN_CONTEXT: A draw nearly eliminates both teams from 3rd-place qualification. This creates an OPEN, ATTACKING match dynamic from both sides. Reduces RULE8's 'fewer goals' assumption. Both teams will press forward.

suspensions.BIH_out: Tarik Muharemovic (CB) — straight red card GD2 vs Switzerland. Hadzikadunic replaces him.
suspensions.QAT_out_1: Homam Al Amin (defender) — red card GD2 vs Canada. Sultan Al Brake replaces.
suspensions.QAT_out_2: Assim Madibo (midfielder) — red card GD2 vs Canada. Karim Boudiaf comes in.
suspensions.significance: Qatar loses more quality — both a defender AND midfielder, while BIH loses only 1 CB.

advancement_scenarios.winner_of_this_match: Goes to 4pts. With 8 of 12 3rd-place teams advancing, 4pts gives realistic 3rd-place shot IF GD improves. BIH needs to win +1 GD (-2 net) or better; QAT needs win AND big margin.
advancement_scenarios.draw: Both stay at 2pts — virtually eliminated. 2pts as 3rd-place team will NOT be in 8 best.
advancement_scenarios.conclusion: Both teams NEED TO WIN. This is functionally a must-win match for both.

BIH_GD1_vs_Canada.bih_goal: Jovo Lukic 21' (header, corner delivery) — Džeko ABSENT (shoulder injury)
BIH_GD1_vs_Canada.note: Creditable draw vs Canada. Lukic deputized for absent Džeko

BIH_GD2_vs_Switzerland.red_card: Tarik Muharemovic — straight red 80' (dangerous tackle on Embolo — 1st red card of WC2026 at that point). SUSPENDED for GD3.
BIH_GD2_vs_Switzerland.note: Dominated by Switzerland. 18 fouls shows Bosnia's combative style when defending. All SUI goals came after 74' (Bosnia red card at 80' compounded).

QAT_GD1_vs_Switzerland.qat_goal: Boualem Khoukhi 90+4' (ACTUAL Qatar goal — header equalizer. NOT an own goal. First ever WC point for Qatar via this goal.)
QAT_GD1_vs_Switzerland.key_observation: Qatar had 0 offsides in 90 minutes of defending vs Switzerland — they do NOT run in behind lines

qat_red_cards: Assim Madibo (midfielder 53', foul on Koné — Koné suffered broken leg)
QAT_GD2_vs_Canada.note: Historic humiliation. Qatar had 2 shots, 0 SOT. Both red-carded players are SUSPENDED for GD3. First AFC team with 2 reds in single WC match.

Edin_Dzeko.international: All-time Bosnia top scorer — 73 goals, 149 caps. Captain.
Edin_Dzeko.gd1_reason: Shoulder injury — Jovo Lukic started as deputy CF

dzeko_sot_calculation.note: GD2 0 SOT was vs Switzerland (real defense). Qatar had 0 SOT allowed vs Canada. Market -110 scorer = very high SOT implied. Discounted to 0.70 for age (40) and uncertainty.

Akram_Afif.note: Not suspended for GD3. Qatar's best player by far. If Qatar attacks, it's through Afif.
Akram_Afif.gd1_sot: Part of Qatar's 3 SOT total vs Switzerland

Jovo_Lukic.role: CF deputy (started when Džeko absent in GD1)
Jovo_Lukic.gd1: Scored vs Canada (21' header from corner)

referee.name: Jesus Valenzuela
referee.wc_experience: Second World Cup (also officiated Qatar 2022 GS + R16)
referee.reputation: Fair, disciplined but not known as extreme card-giver (unlike Abatti)

model_vs_market_gap.BIH_win: Model 47.60% vs Market 69.1% — major gap. Market correctly prices QAT form collapse.
model_vs_market_gap.Draw: Model 29.76% vs Market 18.2% — model overestimates draw; both teams attack
model_vs_market_gap.QAT_win: Model 22.63% vs Market 12.6% — QAT is near RULE7 territory on market basis

Q1.question: Will Bosnia have more 2H corners than Qatar?
key_drivers: Model: P(BIH more 2H corners) = 51.95%
Market BIH -270 = dominant team → more possession → more corners
QAT GD1: only 3 corners vs Switzerland (vs SUI 10); QAT barely attacks
Bosnia attacking team → corners from sustained pressure

Q2.question: Will Bosnia score more goals than Qatar in 2H?
key_drivers: P(BIH 2H goals > QAT 2H goals) from model: ~37-40%
RULE8 fires (draw 29.76%) — draws have equal 2H goals → shade down
However: both teams want to win = more open → offset
Net: slight NO lean; if BIH dominates heavily, this could be closer to 0.48

Q3.question: Will Qatar have more shots on target than Bosnia in 2H?
key_drivers: Model: P(QAT more 2H SOT) = 27.99% — already below 50%
Qatar GD2: 0 SOT total vs Canada (complete attacking shutdown)
Qatar GD1: 3 SOT vs Switzerland but from passive defensive style
Market prices QAT at 12.6% win — near RULE7 threshold
Bosnia will dominate SOT output; Qatar barely generates shots
MAJOR EDGE: crowd likely ~0.37; we're at 0.22. ~15pp NO edge.

Q4.question: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown?
key_drivers: Valenzuela career rate: baseline P(pen OR red) ≈ 0.38
Historical pattern: consistent overpricing of penalty/red card questions
Qatar missing 2 suspended players — remaining players might be cautious about further bans
But: Qatar NEEDS to win → desperation fouls in 2H could produce cards/penalties

Q5.question: Will Qatar commit more fouls than Bosnia?
key_drivers: RULE_FOULS: BIH attacks → QAT defends → QAT fouls more while defending
QAT GD1 vs SUI: 12 fouls while defending (vs SUI 11) — competitive foul rate when trying
QAT GD2 vs CAN: ~1 foul (complete capitulation) — but that was post-2-red-cards
BIH GD2 vs SUI: 18 fouls but they were being dominated (defending team fouls more)
In a match where BIH attacks and QAT defends: QAT expected to foul more

Q6.question: Will Bosnia receive more cards than Qatar?
key_drivers: Qatar defends → Qatar fouls more → Qatar gets more cards
QAT GD2: 2 red cards (though suspended players are now out)
BIH GD2: red card (Muharemovic, also suspended now) + Dedic/Džeko yellows
In GD3: Qatar's desperation defending likely generates more cards than BIH's attacking
DUPLICATE QUESTION WARNING: Q6 and Q7 appear identical. One may be 'Qatar more cards.'

Q7.question: [ASSUMED: Will Qatar receive more cards than Bosnia? — Q6/Q7 appear to be duplicates]
Q7.note: FLAG TO USER: If this IS the same as Q6, use 0.40. If it's the inverse (Qatar more cards), use 0.52.

Q8.question: Will Qatar be caught offside 2 or more times?
key_drivers: Qatar GD1 vs Switzerland: 0 OFFSIDES in 90 minutes of play
Qatar GD2 vs Canada: likely 0-1 (they barely attacked)
Qatar style: set-piece based and defensive. They do NOT run in behind defensive lines.
Against Bosnia's defense, Qatar won't run forward aggressively
Computed probability: P(≥2 | λ=0.8) = 19%. FLOOR_0.25 applied.
MAJOR EDGE: crowd likely ~0.40; we're at 0.25 (floor). ~15pp NO edge.

Q9.question: Will Bosnia and Herzegovina win the match?
key_drivers: Model: P(BIH win) = 47.60%; Market: 69.1% de-vigged
Blend (40% model, 60% market): ~60.5%
Both teams need win → open match → dominant team (BIH) benefits
Qatar depleted: missing 2 suspension players (Al Amin + Madibo)
Bosnia motivated: Džeko finally starting after missing GD1
RULE8 note: effective draw probability lower than model (both teams attack)

Q10.question: Will the match have 2 or fewer total goals?
key_drivers: Model: P(≤2 goals) = 60.99%; Market: ~50% (Over 2.5 at +100)
Both teams attacking = more open play = more goals shade (toward market)
BTTS Yes at -141 (58.5%) from market — high goal probability
Blend: ~52-54% probability of Under 2.5
Uncertain — market and model diverge 11pp. Near coin-flip.

Q11.question: Will Edin Dzeko have at least 1 shot on target?
key_drivers: FanDuel -110 scorer (52% implied) → computed P(≥1 SOT) ≈ 73-91%
Džeko expected to start: age 40, captain, all-time scorer
Qatar allowed 0 SOT total to Canada — Bosnia will dominate shots
GD2 vs SUI: 0 SOT — but that was Switzerland's proper defense
Age discount + starting uncertainty → final estimate 0.70
EDGE: crowd likely ~0.58; our estimate 0.70. ~12pp YES edge.

edge_ranking.note: Qatar more 2H SOT than Bosnia. Qatar 0 SOT in GD2. Model 28%, market QAT 12.6%. Crowd ~0.37.
Qatar 2+ offsides. Qatar 0 offsides in GD1 (90 min!). They don't run in behind. Crowd ~0.40.
Džeko SOT. Market -110 scorer. Qatar 0 SOT defense. Crowd ~0.58.
Bosnia win. Market 69.1%, blend 60.5%. Both need win = open match.
Qatar more fouls. RULE_FOULS defending team direction.
Bosnia more cards than Qatar. Qatar defending = Qatar cards. ~8pp edge.
BIH more 2H goals. Below 50% despite BIH dominance.
BIH more 2H corners. Model 51.95%; dominance adds more.
≤2 goals. Model vs market diverge. Near coin-flip.
Penalty/red card. Valenzuela baseline; historical overpricing.
FLAGGED: possible duplicate of Q6. If duplicate, use 0.40.

post_match.NO_SUBMISSION_FLAG: CRITICAL: Platform shows zero 'YOU' values for every question on this match — only crowd estimate + outcome + a qualitative note ('The Crowd played it safe' / 'The Crowd called it perfectly!'). Total RBP = 0. This means our final_submission_estimates from this file (Q1-Q11 in question_analysis) were computed but NEVER SUBMITTED to the platform before the deadline. This is a pure process failure (missed submission), not a methodology failure — see STEPS_FOR_HIGH_POINTS.md for the full writeup. Estimates below are recorded as 'our_est' from this file purely for retrospective grading (what RBP WOULD have been), not actual submissions.

key_learnings: Process failure: analysis was completed (11 questions modeled in question_analysis) but never submitted to the platform. Zero RBP earned or lost — pure opportunity cost. Root cause unknown (deadline missed, submission step skipped, or platform/session issue) — needs a process safeguard (e.g. a submission checklist or reminder) so a completed analysis never goes unsubmitted again.
